title: aeschynomene egena
intro: Aeschynomene egena, also known as the sensitive plant, is a perennial legume native to Africa and Asia, characterized by its compound leaves that fold up when touched or disturbed. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Aeschynomene egena include relief from fever, pain, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential use in treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Aeschynomene egena include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
